questions.question: What is the primary purpose of Notion Academy as described in the article?

options.text: To sell Notion templates
options.explanation: Selling templates is covered in a specific lesson, not the overall purpose of the Academy.

options.text: To provide a place for live webinars only
options.explanation: Live webinars are one component, but the Academy offers more than just webinars.

options.text: To help users learn Notion, earn badges, and become certified admins
options.explanation: The article states that Notion Academy lets you learn Notion, earn badges, and get certified as an admin.

options.text: To replace the Notion Help Center
options.explanation: The Help Center still exists for quick answers; the Academy is a learning platform, not a replacement.

questions.question: Which path is specifically designed for beginners to gain basic Notion skills?

options.text: Advanced Path
options.explanation: The Advanced Path targets more experienced users.

options.text: Workflows Path
options.explanation: The Workflows Path is intermediate and focuses on connected systems.

options.text: Essentials Path
options.explanation: The Essentials Path is described as for newcomers to learn basic creation, collaboration, and organization.

options.text: Custom Agents Path
options.explanation: Custom Agents is a lesson, not a beginner path.

questions.question: How can a user earn the Notion Certified Admin badge according to the article?

options.text: By completing the Advanced Path only
options.explanation: While the Advanced Path helps, certification requires a specific exam and demonstration of best practices.

options.text: By passing a certification exam that tests workspace design and security best practices
options.explanation: The article says you get certified to run Notion at scale by building smarter workspaces and implementing security best practices.

options.text: By attending any live webinar
options.explanation: Webinars are educational resources but do not grant certification on their own.

options.text: By creating and selling a template in the Notion Marketplace
options.explanation: Template creation is a separate skill and does not lead directly to admin certification.

questions.question: Which lesson focuses on building an intake system using forms, charts, and automations?

options.text: Project management
options.explanation: Project management covers task databases, not intake forms.

options.text: An issue tracker for any team
options.explanation: The issue tracker lesson explicitly mentions building an intake system with forms, charts, and automations.

options.text: Workspace design
options.explanation: Workspace design is about best practices, not specific intake systems.

options.text: Templates & marketplace
options.explanation: That lesson is about creating and selling templates, not intake workflows.

questions.question: Which statement about Notion Academy's live webinars is true?

options.text: Webinars are only available to users on paid Notion plans
options.explanation: The article does not limit webinars to paid plans; they are offered to all learners.

options.text: They include both live sessions and on‑demand recordings to help users improve their skills
options.explanation: The article says webinars are live and on‑demand, designed to help users make the most of Notion.

options.text: Attendance is mandatory to earn any Notion badge
options.explanation: Badges are earned by completing paths/lessons, not by attending webinars.

options.text: All webinars are conducted exclusively in English
options.explanation: While many lessons are listed as English, the article does not state that webinars are English‑only.

questions.question: Considering the structure of Notion Academy, which combination best describes the relationship between “paths”, “lessons”, and “badges”?

options.text: Paths contain multiple lessons; completing lessons within a path can earn you a badge
options.explanation: The article outlines paths made up of lessons, and finishing them lets learners earn badges.

options.text: Badges are prerequisites that must be earned before you can enroll in a path
options.explanation: Badges are the outcome of completing paths, not a prerequisite.

options.text: Lessons are independent of paths and do not contribute to badge acquisition
options.explanation: Lessons are grouped into paths, and completing them contributes toward badges.

options.text: Paths are only available to advanced users, while beginners only receive individual lessons
options.explanation: The article lists beginner paths (Essentials) and shows that paths exist for all skill levels.
